title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: How to Make Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake
easy Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake recipe
authentic Korean Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake
lowCompetitionKeywords: Korean Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake Recipe
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake — a beloved Korean classic

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the outside and tender inside, packed with an array of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This delightful dish offers a perfect balance of oceanic flavors and a satisfying chewiness, making it an irresistible appetizer or light meal. Enjoy it hot, often dipped in a tangy soy-based sauce, for an authentic taste of Korean comfort food.

ingredients.notes: about 10-12 stalks, cut into 3-4 inch pieces
shrimp, squid, mussels, clams, small scallops, cleaned and chopped if large

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, salt, egg, and cold water until a smooth batter forms. The consistency should be similar to a thin pancake batter. Adjust water if needed.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Green Onions
instructions.text: Clean and pat dry all the mixed seafood. If any pieces are too large, cut them into bite-sized portions. Wash and trim the green onions, then cut them into 3-4 inch long pieces.

instructions.title: Make the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, sesame seeds, and minced garlic. Stir well and set aside.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan and Add Batter
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or a cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add about 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the entire pan. Ladle about 1/2 cup of batter into the hot pan, spreading it thinly into a round shape.

instructions.title: Arrange Toppings and Cook First Side
instructions.text: Quickly arrange a generous layer of green onion pieces over the batter, followed by an even scattering of mixed seafood. You can also add sliced red chili if desired. Pour a little more batter over the toppings to help them stick together. Cook for 4-5 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Cook Second Side
instructions.text: Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake. Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. Cook for another 4-5 minutes, pressing down gently with the spatula, until the second side is golden brown and the seafood is fully cooked through.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and slice it into wedges. Repeat the process with the remaining batter and ingredients. Serve hot with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For an extra crispy pancake, use very cold water in the batter and ensure your pan is hot before adding the batter. A small amount of cornstarch or potato starch in the batter also helps.
Feel free to customize the seafood mix. Shrimp, squid, mussels, and scallops are popular choices. Ensure the seafood is fresh and well-drained to prevent a watery pancake.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking for the best texture. If making multiple pancakes, you can keep them warm in a low oven, but they are best fresh off the pan.
article: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake is a beloved Korean Seafood dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
